review_title: Clean, Simple, and Easy to Adopt
description: Overall, Nifty delivers a smooth and reliable project management experience. It strikes a nice balance between structure and simplicity, making it a great option for teams that want clarity, collaboration, and momentum without unnecessary complexity.
author: Jakob F.
pros: The interface is clean and intuitive, making it easy for teams to get started quickly. Tasks, timelines, and conversations live in one place, which reduces tool switching and keeps projects organized without feeling overwhelming.
cons: Reporting and advanced customization are fairly limited. For very complex projects or data-heavy teams, it may feel a bit lightweight compared to more enterprise-focused tools.

review_title: Ideal partner for clunky, growing nonprofit adopting PM tools
description: We were a very high maintenance client for several years, needing a lot of support from the Nifty team, Sky in particular. Our use case is somewhat atypical, so many of the project and template buildouts have needed significant adaptation. The Nifty team has always been extremely supportive, responsive, and clear. We have identified many bugs and gaps over the years as we've grown with Nifty, and they've always resolved them quickly, or given us workarounds if they aren't prioritizing that fix at the moment.
author: Rachel S.
pros: I have a complex organizational structure with 300 employees, many volunteers and contractors, and over 65 departments, all of which need privacy between and among portfolios, but easy transfer of tasks and files between projects and portfolios. When selecting Nifty in 2020, we found that it was the only project management software that met all of our security and privacy needs. Because of this, I'm able for the first time to get a big picture of the activity and progress of all of my teams.
cons: The learning curve, particularly for less tech savvy employees, has been quite high, and at times prohibitive for adoption. Adoption for stragglers has therefore taken 5+ years. However, those who do adopt it use it really consistently, and I'm able to manage those projects with essentially full visibility.

review_title: Think well before you buy
description: Unfortunately the app looks promising but the overall experience wasted a lot of my time and main reason because of customer support.
author: Hossam G.
pros: the user interface is friendly and you can understand it quickly
cons: 1-Their database management and recording user data, for example, have an issue of duplication, and if you created a workspace and then deleted you will not be able to use this name again and the nifty system will keep sending you notifications about It like it still exists. 2-Customer support is not professional and most of the time they take around 4-5 days to reply to your questions. This is the main reason I left the App.

review_title: Powerful Task Management Platform, Backed by a Responsive Team
description: Overall, my experience with Nifty has been very positive, and it’s become a dependable backbone for how we plan and execute work in the business. We still use other tools, but Nifty is where we organize tasks, track progress, share files around specific projects, and keep team communication focused. Once we got past the initial setup and learning curve, it actually reduced confusion and made it easier for everyone to know what they should be working on. The team behind Nifty is responsive, ships regular updates, and has been quick to address questions or suggestions, which gives me confidence to keep expanding how we use it.
author: Robert C.
pros: I run a small business with a lot of moving parts, and Nifty has become the main place where we track tasks, deadlines, and goals throughout the year. The task management is flexible enough to handle everything from one-off to recurring work, and it’s easy to see who owns what and what’s falling behind. The built-in chat and comments help keep conversations tied to tasks instead of lost in email threads, and file sharing inside projects makes it simple to attach the right documents where they’re needed. I also appreciate that they take security seriously and continually improve the platform without bloating it with gimmicks, which makes it very good value for the money.
cons: The main drawback is the learning curve, simply because there are so many ways to set things up and so many features to discover. It took some time to design a workspace that fit how my small team actually works day to day. My biggest early frustration was with Nifty Docs, which felt basic compared to the rest of the platform and lacked version control and document locking, so I chose not to use them much. I shared that feedback with their team, and they’re working on adding those features now, which should make Docs much more useful for things like listing notes and internal procedures that I want to protect.

review_title: Nifty barely covers how awesome this is!
description: Overall, I love Nifty and so do my clients. My clients really enjopy being able to log in and see the progress of the projects and to be able to communicate with me on any portion of the system, be it a task, general chats, or a file.
author: Chris P.
pros: The ease of use and features set. Nifty has a lot of advanced features, but the way they're implemented makes it easy to master them quickly.
cons: At first it was the Milestones. I couldn't get them to work for me the way I thought they should, but once I learned the way Nifty has set them up to be used, they started clicking for me.

review_title: Nifty is an economical and functional PM platform for large or small teams!
description: Overall I've been happy with the Nifty implementation - we have been using it since my company started 6 years ago and we haven't considered switching platforms.
author: Jo N.
pros: As a startup founder with a small team that moves fast, I love Nifty's all-in-one approach for team and project management. It is much less expensive but more functional than other PM solutions.
cons: I wish it had reply functionality in the chat, and that GIFs and emoji reactions worked better in the mobile version.

review_title: They Picked the Right Name!
description: Overall, I'm a big fan of Nifty. I previously used Ora and LOVE it, but they were acquired and will be shutting down soon, so I switched. I tried other platforms (Monday, ClickUp, etc) but Nifty just *works* with the way my brain is organized.
author: Jonathan T.
pros: One thing I like most about Nifty is how it combines so many tools right in one. And I can even embed other tools to further extend what it can do.
cons: One thing I *don't* like is that I can only have one tab of a project open at at time - for example, if I need to get info from a Doc, I have to click over to Docs, but then to update a Todo, I have to leave the Doc and open the Todos.

review_title: Good product
description: It has been a great experience. The free version allows you to do plenty of tasks before you decide to buy, which is a good feature of this application.
author: Stefanny J.
pros: Nifty helped me organize a construction project that I worked on and helped me visualize the main milestone and tasks associated with it. During my experience, I used most of the tools in the free version, which was complete. The paid versions allow you to add more features to complement your management.
cons: At the time I used it, I found it hard to have a macro view of all the tasks, meaning I had to make some tricks in order to get what I wanted, and also had to check some extra videos for that.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Excellent internaol communication
description: A complete solution from the moment you acquire the program; we complete tasks on time with less effort.
author: Saul P.
pros: The platform has everything I need to manage my clients; every purchase, every order, every project is handled ingeniously with excellent internal communication results to deliver a good product to our clients.
cons: Exporting customized reports. I need to check the new version, but I couldn't do it with the previous one.

review_title: Nifty gets project management right
description: Overall, Nifty has helped me manage large projects that I wouldn't be able to handle without the app. I'm able to scale with little effort so I'm very happy with the software.
author: Stacey W.
pros: I'm a fractional marketing consultant. I've been using Nifty for about two years now. The best thing about the software is that I can create complex project management projects with it and share the projects with clients. It's so simple to use that I don't have to teach clients how to work with me within the software. It makes team communication a cinch. That alone makes it worth the money.
cons: Nifty is a powerful tool but the mobile app could be better. Other than that, I don't have any complaints about Nifty.

review_title: Kanban Nifty is Next Level
description: I moved to Nifty when Trello changed its pricing and I have loved it! I have a large team with many remote volunteers so Nifty provides the perfect platform to keep all of their work organized.
author: Skyler R.
pros: I love that I have the options for kanban style boards. I find those so easy to use and organize my projects. I also love that I can invite unlimited members at no extra cost.
cons: I don't like the fact that on mobile you cannot drag the cards to the next board like you can on desktop.

review_title: Project Management tool and Team collobration
description: Over all its good experience but today there are lots of tools in market which is better then this. but i got lifetime deal at $600 so that is plus point that i am still using it.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: There are lots of good project management tool but the main problem is chat with team and project management, nifty has both in one place. so its best tool at the moment when i buy it.
cons: there are still lots if issues, bugs, and system downtime also notification is not working properly. so they need to improve it.

review_title: Boost Your Productivity with Nifty
description: I try to use Nifty in Desktop and Mobile. Also use with another computers, that help me to use a remote desktop to manage tasks, the DOCs help in this way too. We can open a new document in google docs and start from anywhere any project.
author: Jose A.
pros: I used Click Up and Monday, and the similarity is a pro. Bisides that, its a unique app, that help me to boost my productivity. I will try to use the API with N8N to automate some tasks (with Nifty you have the option of automation also).The customer support also is very responsive team with questions.
cons: Portfolios and projects could have some customization to organize, we can only organize with the A-Z. It could be a drag and drop.

review_title: Modern, forward-facing project management with a gorgeous interface
description: I love using Nifty. It is clean, they are always updating the software, and it has been well supported. I enjoy having a task management app that is full-featured but doesn't run slow. 99% of what I need is already there, and I'm finding new features all the time that I get usage out of. The interface, the client portal features, and more make it really enjoyable and flexible for the way I use it, which is to manage client projects and tasks while also having a place for documents and agreements and embedding analytics for clients to see all in one place.
author: Brent R.
pros: I love how well laid out Nifty is. Everything is very clean and easy to see, easy to read, and a joy to use. The site is snappy and not slow like many other tools I've used. Nifty has almost every feature I need, minus a couple of organizational features. But I'm able to manage as many projects as I need, create custom tables and fields, and even have a client portal where I can let clients see and manage tasks, documents, and even embed things like reports, videos and pretty much anything else that can be embedded. I also love how you can use cName so put your whole Nifty account into your own custom domain on your site, which looks better for clients. This is AMAZING in a tool of this price.
cons: I really wish there was one more level of nesting for projects, so I can have multiple projects under a single brand. It's not terrible as I can create multiple portfolios and put them inside it. But one more level would allow me to organize things a little better, which is the only thing I miss from my previous task management app. I would also love to have the ability to embed a pre-sorted "finished tasks" view easily. Right now, it doesn't properly sort tasks when your client visits a shared view. The email system where you can add tasks and documents through email works most of the time, but I've had it not add things a few times.

review_title: Better than most out of the gate
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: This is the easiest-on-the-eyes, most intuitive PM platform we've tried or used (especially compared to Asana and Notion). Document storage, creation, and management is a breeze. Uploading large numbers of files is lightning fast, and document navigation is super easy. Task management has deep capacity. Onboarding has been a snap compared to other platforms. Devs have built in heavy emphasis on real-time communication and collaboration. Support is super responsive. They have a very active development schedule. Nifty will replace Asana, Slack, and Dropbox Paper for us.
cons: There is no native print function anywhere. All uploaded files and files created in Nifty have to be downloaded in order to print. This presents companies with an added security issue. In the Files section, pdfs are uploaded as images, so even if you download to print, you just have a png. The Share function for Files only shares to people on your team or onboarded guests. All kinds of system notifications show up in the Discussion streams and there's no way to delete them. The functionality of the Discussions widget is less than the functionality when you click through and expand the Discussions to a full window. So, for instance, you cannot pin a discussion from the widget. This is the least intuitive thing about the app.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Nifty is a very good project management system with a lot of potential.
description: Nifty has been useful in helping us more effectively track our projects with their simple Trello-like board, track our hours per tasks and projects, and streamline communication.
author: Eric R B.
pros: The pricing was attractive and the fact that it allowed for a good number of internal and external collaborators worked out well for us.
cons: The UI feels like it would benefit from a review by a UX expert to reduce the number of clicks to select things and do basic actions.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Good PMS with fair pricing
author: Rémy G.
pros: Nifty is flexible and can be adapted easily to every business use case.
cons: I am not a huge fan of per member payment, even though niftys price is fair and low.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Really easy to use project management tool
author: Grant M.
pros: Nifty is really competitively priced and has a lot of features for small and larger businesses. The UI is easy to navigate and it has a dedicated desktop app so you don't need to have yet another tab open in your browser. There's a generous free plan for people who don't have complex needs or just want to try it out, but the subscription cost isn't excessive for full features.
cons: I'm a beginner with using Nifty, and although there are a lot of FAQ guides which cater to many different use cases, it still takes time to figure out exactly how to set everything up. Once everything is up and running, though, it works well.

review_title: Revolutionizing Project Management
description: My overall experience with Nifty has been extremely positive. The platform offers a comprehensive set of features that address various aspects of project management, making it a reliable and efficient tool for teams. From the moment I started exploring Nifty, I was impressed by its user-friendly interface, which made navigation and task management a breeze. The intuitive design allowed me to quickly grasp the platform's functionalities and start using it effectively.One of the highlights of my experience was Nifty's emphasis on collaboration. The seamless integration of collaboration tools and the ability to leave comments, tag team members, and share files within the project workspace greatly enhanced communication and teamwork. Real-time updates and notifications kept everyone informed, ensuring that tasks were completed efficiently and project progress was transparent.Nifty's project planning and scheduling capabilities were also commendable. The intuitive drag-and-drop interface and the ability to set task dependencies made it easy to create and manage projects effectively. The Gantt chart view provided a visual representation of project timelines and task dependencies, enabling better resource allocation and project planning.Additionally, Nifty's integration with other popular tools added immense value to my experience. The ability to connect with applications like Google Drive and Slack streamlined workflows and eliminated the need to switch between multiple platf
author: Sofwane S.
pros: One of the aspects I liked most about Nifty is its seamless collaboration features. The platform provides a range of tools that facilitate effective communication and teamwork within project teams. The ability to leave comments, tag team members, and share files directly within the project workspace creates a collaborative environment where ideas can be exchanged, feedback can be provided, and project-related discussions can take place in a centralized location. This promotes transparency, enhances productivity, and ensures that everyone is on the same page. The real-time updates and notifications further contribute to efficient collaboration, keeping team members informed about task assignments, changes, and completions. Overall, Nifty's dedication to fostering collaboration and streamlining teamwork stood out as a notable strength of the platform.
cons: While Nifty impressed me with its comprehensive features, there was one aspect that I found could be improved. The platform's mobile application experience was not as robust as its web version. While the web interface provided a seamless and user-friendly experience, the mobile app felt slightly limited in terms of functionality and responsiveness. Some features that were easily accessible on the web platform were not as intuitive or readily available on the mobile app. Enhancing the mobile app's functionality and ensuring a smoother user experience would greatly benefit users who rely on mobile devices for project management on the go. Despite this minor drawback, the overall performance and capabilities of Nifty remained highly commendable.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Maximizing Efficiency and amazing alternative for Asana
description: Firstly, the ability to create tasks and invite partners to collaborate is a key feature that sets your platform apart. It makes it easy for teams to work together on a project, keeping everyone in the loop and ensuring that tasks are completed on time.The time tracking feature is a game-changer. It makes it easy to monitor progress and keep track of how much time is being spent on each task. This helps ensure that projects are completed on time and within budget, and it also provides valuable insights into how the team is working.Overall, your project management platform is absolutely amazing. It offers all of the key features that are essential for effective project management, and it does so in a way that is user-friendly and easy to use. Whether you are working on a small project or a large-scale project, this platform is the perfect tool to help you stay organized and on track. I would highly recommend it to anyone looking for a powerful project management tool.
author: Codrut I.
pros: The ability to create tasks and invite partners to collaborate is an excellent feature that enables effective communication among team members.The time tracking feature is incredibly valuable and provides useful insights into how much time is being spent on each task.The platform is user-friendly and easy to use, making it accessible for team members with varying levels of technical expertise.The platform offers a wide range of customization options that allow teams to tailor their workflows to their unique needs and preferences.
cons: The platform's extensive customization options can be overwhelming for some users, particularly those who are new to project management software.While the time tracking feature is valuable, it may not be necessary for all projects, and some users may find it distracting or cumbersome.The platform's pricing structure can be a bit confusing, particularly for teams that are new to project management software.On the mobile side there it's work to be done.

review_title: Nifty kinda does it all, and does it well.
description: I work for a fully remote corporate level e-learning developer and we work across multiple clients and projects/portfolio's at any given time. Nifty handles the needs of our team to work collaboratively across projects with different milestone types and requirements with ease. I would highly recommend it to any remote team or otherwise.
author: Jason C.
pros: Nifty has so many features and constant updates unlock more and more features which is great value and functionality. Manage projects and work flows with complete confidence
cons: It's not a file management system or wiki... so use something else to do your database work.

review_title: Nifty's Review as a Daily User
description: My overall experience with Nifty has been highly positive. The platform provides a comprehensive solution for project management, allowing teams to efficiently plan, execute, and track projects from start to finish. The collaborative features, such as real-time chat and file sharing, have significantly improved communication and streamlined the project workflow. Nifty has become an indispensable tool for my team, enabling us to stay organized, meet deadlines, and achieve our project goals effectively.
author: Jersey E.
pros: What I liked most about Nifty is its seamless integration of multiple project management features in one platform. From task management to project planning, scheduling, and real-time collaboration, Nifty covers all the essential aspects of project management in a user-friendly and intuitive interface. It streamlines workflow, promotes effective communication, and enhances team collaboration, making it a valuable tool for project success.
cons: While Nifty offers a wide range of features, the learning curve for some advanced functionalities can be a bit steep. It may take some time for users to fully grasp and utilize all the capabilities that Nifty has to offer. Additionally, occasional minor glitches or delays in loading times have been observed, although they have not significantly impacted the overall user experience.

review_title: Amazing project management software
description: I simply love Nifty. It is my day to day management tool and makes my life so much easier. I would say is something in between Trello and Asana. It is more much more advanced than Trello but keeping it's simplicity and has pretty much the same features than Asana but with a better UX.
author: Diego D.
pros: You can change views to see your tasks in a Kaban view, task list, milestones, Gannt project view, etc. But the best thing is that you can have a calendar view with all the tasks of the day/week/month. It has a really good UX and a mobile app to manage everything from your phone. It allows you to see either the teams workload or only your workload which can be especially useful for project managers. It also has many collaborative speaces. You can have conversations either at a task level or at the project level. You can share there links, files, resources... and it also has a specific workspace to share files and have all the project files that you need in one space.
cons: Overall it has a great UX but there's a few things that could be improved. The calendar view is still a bit basic. I miss having tasks in different colors to prioritise and time-block properly. However, the product has a roadmap and is constantly impoving so I'm sure this is something we will see soon.

review_title: Excellent tool for project management and communication
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I use it daily for communicating with my team on multiple projects, planning sprints and executing deliveries like a boss. This is one of the most underrated tool.
cons: The only thing missing is to apply multiple tags to cards at once which needs to there.

review_title: Easy Project Management tool
author: Khevana P.
pros: Ease of use and onboard a new user. Other tools are sometimes too technical to train the users who are non-technical but Nifty is easy to adopt and customise.
cons: Nothing that I can think of at this stage.
